user_query:
I'm polishing my student blog about space plasmas and Hannes Alfvén, and I want a data-driven SEO pass that prioritizes what to optimize first and gives me concrete on-page updates. Please use the provided local drafts and keyword list, and also perform live web searches. Deliverables must be created under the output/ directory as specified.

Inputs to use:
- input/keywords.csv (maps target keywords to a local draft file)
- input/drafts/*.md (markdown drafts to analyze)
- input/site_info.yaml (site name and persona context)

What to do:
1) For each keyword in input/keywords.csv, perform a web search using a general-purpose search engine (any widely-used public search engine is fine). For each query:
   - Capture the top 10 organic results (skip ads), extracting: rank (1..10), result title, URL, and domain.
   - Save a snapshot JSON to output/serp/<keyword_slug>.json where <keyword_slug> is the normalized keyword: lowercase, replace spaces with hyphens, and strip non-alphanumeric/hyphen characters. JSON structure must be:
     {
       "keyword": "...",
       "captured_at_iso": "YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ",
       "results": [
         {"rank": 1, "title": "...", "url": "...", "domain": "example.com"},
         ... up to 10 results ...
       ]
     }
   - Define a result as "authoritative" if its domain TLD is .edu or .gov, or the registered domain matches any of: nasa.gov, esa.int, ieee.org, sciencedirect.com, springer.com, wiley.com, nature.com.

2) Read local drafts in input/drafts and extract for each mapped post in keywords.csv:
   - H1 title (the first line starting with #) as the page title.
   - Full text to compute:
     - word_count (split on whitespace)
     - keyword_occurrences (case-insensitive count of the exact keyword string from keywords.csv)
     - title_contains_keyword (1 if H1 contains the exact keyword case-insensitively, else 0)

3) Build a prioritization table and save it as output/analysis/keyword_prioritization.csv with these columns, one row per keyword in input/keywords.csv:
   - keyword
   - target_post (from CSV)
   - post_file_exists (true/false)
   - word_count (integer; 0 if file missing)
   - keyword_occurrences (integer; 0 if file missing)
   - title_contains_keyword (0/1)
   - authoritative_domains_in_top10 (integer 0..10, from the SERP snapshot)
   - content_readiness_score (float 0..1 rounded to 3 decimals), computed as:
       content_readiness_score = 0.5 * min(keyword_occurrences, 5)/5 + 0.3 * (1 if word_count >= 300 else 0) + 0.2 * (1 if title_contains_keyword == 1 else 0)
   - competition_score (integer) = authoritative_domains_in_top10
   - opportunity_score (float 0..1 rounded to 4 decimals), computed as:
       opportunity_score = (1 - (competition_score/10)) * 0.6 + content_readiness_score * 0.4
   Sort the CSV by opportunity_score descending. Exclude rows where post_file_exists is false from the sort, but still include them at the end with zeroed metrics.

4) Save the top 3 rows by opportunity_score (post_file_exists must be true) to output/analysis/top3_opportunities.csv, preserving the same columns.

5) For those top 3 opportunities, generate on-page suggestions and save to output/recommendations/onpage_suggestions.csv with columns:
   - target_post
   - keyword
   - recommended_title (must include the exact keyword, <= 60 characters; if the site_name from input/site_info.yaml can be appended as " | <site_name>" without exceeding 60 chars, you may append it; otherwise omit)
   - recommended_meta_description (must include the exact keyword, 140–160 characters inclusive)
   - recommended_h1 (must include the exact keyword, <= 60 characters)
   - title_length (integer)
   - meta_description_length (integer)
   Validate the length constraints in the file (fill the length columns based on your outputs).

6) For each top-3 target_post, write an updated copy to output/draft_updates/<original_filename>.md that preserves the original content but inserts a YAML front matter block at the top with:
   ---
   seo_title: "<recommended_title>"
   meta_description: "<recommended_meta_description>"
   ---
   If a front matter already exists, add or update only these two fields.

Operational notes and constraints:
- All three operations are essential: you must read and analyze the provided local drafts and CSV; perform live search engine queries to build SERP snapshots; and filter, sort, and rank the keywords by the specified opportunity_score formula.
- Do not hardcode any particular search engine URL; use a widely-available general-purpose engine and extract titles/URLs/domains from the live results you receive.
- The task is complete when the following artifacts exist and are consistent with each other:
  - output/serp/*.json (one per keyword with the specified schema)
  - output/analysis/keyword_prioritization.csv (sorted with computed scores per formula)
  - output/analysis/top3_opportunities.csv (subset of the above)
  - output/recommendations/onpage_suggestions.csv (meeting the keyword inclusion and length constraints)
  - output/draft_updates/*.md (for the top 3, containing the specified front matter)

Please keep file and column names exactly as specified so I can review diffs and run checks locally. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
